title: Syracuse expands Surge Link broadband to more underserved neighborhoods
summary: Syracuse is expanding Surge Link, its municipal broadband service, to 9,200+ additional households in underserved neighborhoods (Valley, Skunk City, Washington Square, Northside, Prospect Hill, Hawley-Green) through a $15M fixed-wireless infrastructure investment supported by a $10.8M ConnectALL grant from Governor Hochul. The service offers 100 Mbps at $14.99/month (low-income) or $36.99/month with no contracts, caps, or rental fees, positioning digital equity as essential infrastructure for education, healthcare, and economic opportunity.

political_signal.description: Mayor publicly framing broadband access as essential infrastructure (not luxury); significant state partnership ($10.8M grant) signals alignment with NY ConnectALL priorities; municipal service model positions equity and affordability as policy goals.

processing_notes: Dollar amounts validated: $15M total project investment, $10.8M state grant confirmed; monthly pricing ($14.99 low-income, $36.99 standard) extracted
Six neighborhoods explicitly named as expansion targets for CIO neighborhood-level context
Service partner (Community Broadband Networks) identified for procurement/contract context
Governor Hochul credit suggests intergovernmental coordination opportunity
No departmental attribution in article—recommend confirmation of lead department (likely CIO or equivalent innovation/digital services function)
